items.title: Nasdaq drops 1% as chip stocks tumble and oil spikes; Dow slips below 53,000
items.content: Dow closed at 52,925.15 (-0.25%), S&P 500 at 7,503.85 (-0.45%), Nasdaq Composite dropped 1.16% to 25,818.69. Chip stocks led declines amid rout extending from Asia.

items.title: SpaceX falls 5% on fast-track addition to Nasdaq-100
items.content: SpaceX shares declined 5% following accelerated inclusion into the Nasdaq-100 index.

items.title: Dow surpasses 53K; sets new closing record after holiday weekend
items.content: Dow Jones Industrial Average finished at a record high above 53,000 on July 6, while weakness in chip and technology stocks weighed on the Nasdaq.

items.title: Dow jumps nearly 600 points to record close; Nasdaq slides as chip stocks suffer
items.content: Dow gained 594.83 points (+1.14%) to close at 52,900.07. S&P 500 virtually flat at 7,483.24, Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.8% to 25,832.67.

items.title: Hong Kong Hang Seng closes at 23,461.33, rallied 1.89% on July 7
items.content: Hang Seng gained 434.65 points to close at 23,461.33. Session high 23,820.92, low 23,398.27. Index up 2.54% over past 5 sessions, but still 16.4% below 52-week high of 28,056.10.

items.title: Hang Seng Index extends rally on biotech and internet stock strength
items.content: Hong Kong stocks pushed higher on Monday July 6, with Hang Seng extending outperformance relative to regional peers as investors rotated into HK stocks.

items.title: US and Iran agree to continue discussions, host and mediator Qatar says
items.content: US and Iran reached initial agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz and extend ceasefire, potentially allowing oil and gas to reach global markets.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis update
items.content: Iran re-closed strait on June 20 citing Israeli violations. US Navy announced widened route through Strait on June 27. Ceasefire talks continuing through Qatar.

items.title: Measurement tweaks will make inflation data look better
items.content: Core PCE inflation at 3.4% for 12 months ended in May, above Fed's 2% target every month since March 2021. Upcoming measurement methodology changes expected to lower reported inflation.

items.title: US PCE price index expected to rise 0.5% MoM in May 2026
items.content: May PCE expected to accelerate to 0.5% MoM from 0.4% in April, driven by higher energy costs linked to Iran conflict. Headline PCE at 4.07% YoY.

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
items.content: SMIC Hong Kong shares fell more than 11%, Hua Hong Grace also tumbled. Asian semiconductor stocks hit hard by global sell-off.

items.title: Macquarie says now is 'best time' to buy Chinese AI chip stocks
items.content: Macquarie analysts bullish on Chinese AI chip firms citing government support and import restrictions on Nvidia GPUs boosting domestic leaders like Huawei Ascend.
